column1: Deterministic rules for the MODIFY_AND_CONTINUE branch. When screening routes a user to MODIFY (a joint or soft-tissue flag the follow-ups confirmed is minor), the generator reads the row for the flagged region and applies it to every session. This is a fixed lookup, not model judgement: the same flag always produces the same constraints. The user's free-text 'which movements make it worse' adds nuance on top, but the floor below is guaranteed. exclude_ids are removed from selection entirely; downgrade means the exercise stays eligible only if no excluded_free alternative fills the slot, and then at raised RIR. All flagged-region exercises get rir_bump added to rir_min. This never overrides a HARD_SAFETY rule or a REQUIRE_PROFESSIONAL_CLEARANCE outcome.

column3: Hinge (heavy), loaded spinal flexion/rotation under load
column4: Supported=Yes, machine and chest-supported variations, hip-dominant work without spinal load
column7: HG01, BK12, BK13, QD01, QD06, HG07, HG08, FB01, FB02, FB07, FB11, CO14
column9: The big free-weight hinges and standing barbell work come out first. Keep training the muscles through supported and machine options: chest-supported rows, leg press, hip thrust, back extension held short of end range.

column3: Deep knee flexion under load, high-impact jumping, plyometrics
column4: Supported=Yes, machine leg work, partial-range and hip-dominant options, Impact=Low only
column7: QD01, QD06, QD03, QD04, QD08, QD11, QD10, QD14, FB05, FB06, FB09
column9: Cut jumping and deep loaded squats. Keep quads and hamstrings via leg press in a comfortable range, leg extension and curl machines, and hinge patterns that spare the knee.

column3: Overhead pressing, wide-grip and deep pressing, behind-neck work
column4: Neutral-grip and supported presses, rows over overhead work, partial range, Impact=Low
column7: SH02, SH07, SH11, CH01, CH09, CH03, TR01, TR05, TR06, BK04, FB03, FB04, FB09
column9: Overhead and heavy flat pressing come out first. Keep chest and shoulders through neutral-grip dumbbell presses, machine presses in a pain-free range, and plenty of pulling. Rear-delt and rotator-friendly work is encouraged.

column3: Loaded wrist extension, straight-bar pressing and curling, push-up positions
column4: Neutral-grip and machine options, straps or handles that keep the wrist neutral, avoid full weight-bearing on the hand
column7: CH01, CH09, TR01, SH01, BI01, TR05
column9: Take out push-up positions and straight-bar work that force wrist extension. Machines, neutral-grip dumbbells and cables let the user keep training with a neutral wrist.

column3: Deep hip flexion under load, wide stance, explosive hinging
column4: Supported and machine work, moderate range, controlled tempo, Impact=Low
column7: QD01, QD06, HG01, HG07, HG08, QD03, QD04, QD11, HG13
column9: Cut explosive and deep-range hip work. Keep legs and glutes through machine and supported options in a comfortable range, and single-leg work only if pain-free.

column4: Low-impact conditioning (bike, rower, swim), seated calf work, stable-stance lifts
column7: QD03, QD04, QD08, QD11, FB05, FB06, CA01, CA03
column9: Remove jumping and running-based conditioning. Swap to bike or rower for cardio, seated calf raises, and lifts taken from a stable stance. Standing calf and lunges return once pain-free.

column1: How the generator applies a row
1. Screening + follow-ups route the user to MODIFY_AND_CONTINUE and record the affected region(s) from the Step 7 injury chip and the 'which movements make it worse' free text.

2. For each flagged region, load its row here. Remove every exclude_id from the candidate pool. Mark every downgrade_id as last-resort (used only if a slot cannot otherwise be filled, and then at rir_min + rir_bump).

3. Within every remaining slot, prefer candidates matching the 'prefer' column (Supported=Yes, Impact=Low, machine/neutral-grip as specified). Add rir_bump to rir_min for any exercise whose Stress Regions include matches_stress_region.

4. If removing exclude_ids empties a required slot, walk the Substitutions list to the first compatible, non-excluded exercise. A required slot is never left unfilled; it is filled with a safer option.
5. The free-text answer refines but never loosens the row: it can exclude more, never re-include an exclude_id.

6. review_after: the modifications lift only when the user reports the region symptom-free for the stated period. Until then they persist on every regenerated session. Re-flagging the region resets the clock.

7. Multiple regions stack: apply every flagged row. If the combined exclusions make a goal's volume impossible, reduce volume before re-including any excluded exercise, and tell the user their program is modified for their injuries.

Scope limit: this sheet handles minor, self-managed flags that screening cleared for training. Anything painful now, under treatment, exercise-restricted or still active routes to REQUIRE_PROFESSIONAL_CLEARANCE and no program generates. These modification rules are a starting safety floor and must be reviewed by an accredited exercise professional before launch.
